content: If we extend the Merriam-Webster definition of an agent as "any entity that exerts power to produce an effect" to include biological pathogenic agents, we can hypothesize that ICM curiosity mechanisms work like biological infectious agents: the intrinsic reward for novel states acts like a pathogen’s virulence factor, spreading the agent’s exploratory behavior into uncolonized regions of the environment, just as a biological agent spreads into a new host.
